import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  mut have_digit = false
  mut neg = false
  mut val = 0
  while i <= n
    mut b = 32
    if i < n
      match string.byte_at(s, i)
      when Some(x)
        b = x
      when None
        b = 32
      end
    end
    if b >= 48 and b <= 57
      val = val * 10 + (b - 48)
      have_digit = true
    else if b == 45 and not have_digit and not neg
      neg = true
    else
      if have_digit
        let v = if neg
          -val
        else
          val
        end
        result = list.push(result, v)
      end
      have_digit = false
      neg = false
      val = 0
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  result
end

fn get_at(_ xs: List<Int>, _ i: Int) -> Int
  match xs.get(i)
  when Some(v)
    v
  when None
    0
  end
end

fn remove_at(_ xs: List<Int>, _ idx: Int) -> List<Int>
  mut out: List<Int> = []
  mut k = 0
  let n = xs.len()
  while k < n
    if k != idx
      out = list.push(out, get_at(xs, k))
    end
    k = k + 1
  end
  out
end

fn sort_desc(_ xs: List<Int>) -> List<Int>
  mut input = xs
  mut output: List<Int> = []
  while input.len() > 0
    mut max_val = get_at(input, 0)
    mut max_idx = 0
    mut j = 1
    let n = input.len()
    while j < n
      let cur = get_at(input, j)
      if cur > max_val
        max_val = cur
        max_idx = j
      end
      j = j + 1
    end
    output = list.push(output, max_val)
    input = remove_at(input, max_idx)
  end
  output
end

let line1 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let line2 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let nums = parse_ints(line1)
let k_nums = parse_ints(line2)
let k = get_at(k_nums, 0)

let sorted = sort_desc(nums)
let total = sorted.len()
let limit = if k < total
  k
else
  total
end

mut out = ""
mut i = 0
while i < limit
  let v = get_at(sorted, i)
  if i == 0
    out = "#{v}"
  else
    out = "#{out} #{v}"
  end
  i = i + 1
end
println(out)
